evals.prompt: Can you prove that the sum of the first 1000 prime numbers is itself a prime number?
evals.expected_output: DISPROVED — the sum 3,682,913 is composite (13 × 29 × 9,769). Proof script uses sympy, passes all validation checks.
expectations: The proof correctly identifies the sum as 3,682,913
The proof finds that 3,682,913 is NOT prime and outputs DISPROVED
The proof provides a factorization as counterexample
A CLAIM_FORMAL dict with operator_note is present
The proof script is self-contained and runnable via python proof.py
validate_proof.py passes with no issues
proof.py includes FACT_REGISTRY with A-prefixed IDs
proof.py __main__ emits === PROOF SUMMARY (JSON) === block
JSON summary contains fact_registry (A-types with method/result), claim_formal, verdict, key_results
Three output files: proof.py, proof.md, proof_audit.md
proof.md has executive summary with key numbers under verdict
proof.md uses inline fact references (A1, A2)
proof_audit.md contains Type A facts table, computation traces, and hardening checklist
proof_audit.md omits Type B sections (pure-math proof)

evals.prompt: Is it really true that the State of Israel is over 70 years old? Prove it rigorously.
evals.expected_output: PROVED (with unverified citations) — Israel founded May 14, 1948, age is 77 years as of March 2026, which is > 70.
expectations: Two independent sources are cited for the founding date
Dates are parsed from quotes using parse_date_from_quote, not hand-typed
Citations are verified using verify_all_citations which returns structured dicts
date.today() is used for system time anchoring
CLAIM_FORMAL has operator '>' with operator_note explaining the choice
At least 3 adversarial checks are structurally independent
Cross-check confirms both sources agree on the founding date
Verdict is PROVED or PROVED (with unverified citations)
validate_proof.py passes with no issues
proof.py includes FACT_REGISTRY mapping B1, B2 to source keys
proof.py __main__ emits === PROOF SUMMARY (JSON) === block
JSON summary citations use structured dict fields (status/method/coverage_pct/fetch_mode), not parsed from message strings
Three output files: proof.py, proof.md, proof_audit.md
proof.md has executive summary with key numbers under verdict
proof.md uses inline fact references (B1, B2)
proof_audit.md contains full evidence table with quotes and URLs from JSON citations
proof_audit.md citation details show status, method, fetch_mode, and impact
proof_audit.md contains CLAIM_FORMAL specification and FACT_REGISTRY from JSON summary
proof_audit.md contains computation traces reproduced from explain_calc inline output
proof_audit.md sections labeled with provenance
proof_audit.md hardening checklist is complete
proof.md conclusion states impact of any unverified citations

evals.prompt: Prove that Python is the best programming language
evals.expected_output: Skill correctly identifies this as an opinion/value judgment and declines to produce a proof. Offers factual alternatives.
expectations: No proof script is generated
The response identifies the claim as a value judgment or opinion
The response explains that 'best' has no objective definition without criteria
The response offers factual alternatives that could be proven

evals.prompt: Fact-check this: Earth's average temperature has risen by more than 1 degree Celsius since 1880.
evals.expected_output: PROVED or PROVED (with unverified citations) — multiple sources confirm ~1.1C warming. Proof uses multiple independent sources, some may have partial citation verification due to page formatting.
expectations: At least 3 independent empirical sources cited (NOAA, NASA, IPCC, etc.)
FACT_REGISTRY has B1, B2, B3+ entries, each mapping to a distinct source key
Each source is its own fact row in proof.md Evidence Summary
JSON summary citations have structured fields (status/method/coverage_pct/fetch_mode) per fact
JSON summary extractions object has entries for each extracted value
proof_audit.md shows per-source citation verification with status, method, and fetch_mode
Any partial citation is clearly distinguished from verified in proof_audit.md
proof_audit.md Citation Verification Details has impact field for non-verified citations labeled as author analysis
proof.md Proof Logic references fact IDs inline and notes source agreement where applicable
proof.md conclusion addresses any unverified citations with impact on specific conclusions
Fact IDs are consistent across proof.py JSON summary, proof.md, and proof_audit.md
proof_audit.md extraction records include method narrative labeled as author analysis
proof_audit.md computation traces reproduced verbatim from explain_calc inline output
Three output files: proof.py, proof.md, proof_audit.md
validate_proof.py passes with no issues

evals.prompt: Prove that the population of Tokyo exceeded 13 million by 2020. Note: Python may not have internet access in this environment, so include page snapshots in empirical_facts.
evals.expected_output: PROVED or PROVED (verified from snapshots) — population data confirmed from provided snapshots of authoritative sources.
expectations: empirical_facts entries include snapshot field with pre-fetched page text
verify_citation uses snapshot when live fetch is unavailable
JSON summary citations show fetch_mode for each citation (live or snapshot)
proof_audit.md clearly indicates verification mode per citation (live vs snapshot)
FACT_REGISTRY maps fact IDs to source keys
Values parsed from quotes using parse_number_from_quote, not hand-typed
verify_extraction called on extracted values
Three output files: proof.py, proof.md, proof_audit.md
validate_proof.py passes with no issues

evals.prompt: Is the speed of light faster than the speed of sound by more than a factor of 1 million?
evals.expected_output: DISPROVED — the ratio is approximately 874,000x, which is less than 1,000,000. Speed of light ~299,792,458 m/s, speed of sound ~343 m/s.
expectations: Both speeds are cited from authoritative sources with exact quotes
Values are parsed from quotes, not hand-typed
The ratio is computed in Python, not stated by the LLM
CLAIM_FORMAL has operator '>' with threshold 1000000 and operator_note
Verdict is DISPROVED because ~874,000 < 1,000,000
Adversarial check searches for whether the ratio could exceed 1 million under different conditions
validate_proof.py passes with no issues
Three output files: proof.py, proof.md, proof_audit.md

evals.prompt: Prove that Brazil is both the largest country in South America by area AND has the largest population in South America.
evals.expected_output: PROVED — Brazil leads South America in both area (~8.5M km²) and population (~203M+). Compound AND claim with both sub-claims verified.
expectations: CLAIM_FORMAL includes sub_claims list with conjunction AND
At least two sub-claims: area comparison and population comparison
Each sub-claim has its own sources and extracted values
At least one comparison country cited for each dimension
Values parsed from quotes using parse_number_from_quote
Both sub-claims must be PROVED for overall PROVED verdict
validate_proof.py passes with no issues
Three output files: proof.py, proof.md, proof_audit.md

evals.prompt: Is it true that coffee consumption reduces type 2 diabetes risk?
evals.expected_output: PARTIALLY VERIFIED or UNDETERMINED — studies show associations but with conflicting magnitudes, confounders, and no definitive causal proof.
expectations: Multiple sources cited showing different study conclusions
The proof acknowledges conflicting or nuanced evidence
Verdict is PARTIALLY VERIFIED or UNDETERMINED, not PROVED
The proof explains why a definitive verdict cannot be reached
Adversarial check includes studies showing limitations or confounders
validate_proof.py passes with no issues
Three output files: proof.py, proof.md, proof_audit.md

evals.prompt: Prove that the Eiffel Tower is taller than the Statue of Liberty, which is taller than the Leaning Tower of Pisa.
evals.expected_output: PROVED — Eiffel Tower (~330m) > Statue of Liberty (~93m) > Leaning Tower of Pisa (~56m). Multi-hop transitive chain.
expectations: Three separate structures cited with height data from independent sources
Heights parsed from quotes, not hand-typed
Two explicit comparisons: Eiffel > Statue of Liberty, Statue of Liberty > Leaning Tower
CLAIM_FORMAL captures the transitive chain structure
All three heights cross-checked against at least two sources each or adversarial check confirms no contradicting heights
validate_proof.py passes with no issues
Three output files: proof.py, proof.md, proof_audit.md

evals.prompt: Will AI surpass human intelligence by 2030?
evals.expected_output: Skill correctly identifies this as a future prediction and returns UNDETERMINED or declines to produce a proof.
expectations: The response identifies the claim as a future prediction
The response explains that future events cannot be verified
Verdict is UNDETERMINED if a proof is generated, or no proof is generated
No PROVED or DISPROVED verdict is issued

evals.prompt: Prove that the 50th Fibonacci number is divisible by 5 but not by 7.
expectations: The proof computes Fib(50) correctly as 12586269025
Both divisibility checks are computed in Python
CLAIM_FORMAL captures the compound AND structure (divisible by 5 AND not divisible by 7)
No empirical sources needed — pure Type A computation
Adversarial check verifies Fibonacci computation independently (e.g., closed-form or alternative algorithm)
validate_proof.py passes with no issues

expectations: At least two independent sources cited (IMF, World Bank, OECD, etc.)
Percentage values parsed from quotes using parse_percentage_from_quote or parse_number_from_quote
CLAIM_FORMAL has operator '>' with threshold 200 and operator_note

evals.prompt: Fact-check: The Great Wall of China is visible from space with the naked eye.
evals.expected_output: DISPROVED — NASA and astronaut testimony confirm the Great Wall is not visible from low Earth orbit with the naked eye.
expectations: At least one authoritative source (NASA, astronaut accounts) cited with exact quote
The proof identifies this as a common misconception
Verdict is DISPROVED
